title: Polymath capability provider binding
description: Validates a `bindings/<provider>/binding.json` inside a connector or infra plugin. A binding is the AUTHORING unit for 'this plugin supplies <provider> for <capability>'. tools/build-capability-index.py reads every binding and GENERATES the `providerPlugins{}` map in shared/schemas/capabilities.json, so that map can never be aspirational — a provider is wired iff a binding exists. The providing plugin is derived from the file path (plugins/<plugin>/bindings/<provider>/binding.json), so a binding cannot claim the wrong plugin. Runtime packaging (how/whether the MCP server launches) is intentionally NOT decided here — see docs/plans/consolidation-and-dispatch.md Phase 2.

provider.description: Provider id, lower_snake_case. Must appear in this capability's `providers[]` vocabulary in capabilities.json (add it there first to recognise a new provider).

capability.description: The capability this binding supplies. Closed vocabulary, mirrors capabilities.schema.json.

transport.description: How the provider is reached. 'mcp' = an MCP server (named by `server`, defined in the plugin's .mcp.json today; how it is packaged so only the configured one launches is the deferred Phase 2 decision). 'cli' = a local vendor CLI. 'skill' = no server/CLI; the plugin contributes design/workflow skills only (e.g. infra design plugins).

server.description: For kind=mcp: the mcpServers key in the plugin's .mcp.json that serves this provider. Multiple providers may share one server (e.g. github_issues/github_actions/dependabot all ride the github server).

command.description: For kind=cli: the vendor CLI binary (e.g. terraform, kubectl).

userConfigKeys.description: plugin.json userConfig keys this provider needs (credentials / endpoints). Used to validate that a binding's provider is actually configurable, and by future packaging to scope secrets per provider.

references.description: references/*.md tool docs for this provider.
